exact_statement: Generative physical roots admit exact aligned fields, checked indices, checked byte ranges, and disjoint split or join without implying liveness.
derivation: Create generative names; derive checked places and exact cover; seal pre-existing leaf authority without adding an owner.
deletion_result: Deleting names, places, partition, or leaf sealing forces eager initialization, per-element roots, copies, backpointers, or unsafe wrong-root access.
falsifier: The derivation creates liveness from a place or needs a runtime root/version object.

exact_statement: A closed grammar describes vacant and live atoms, spans, finite products and sums, metadata-classified roles, and strict owned children under exact roots and generations.
derivation: Seal exact leaves; compose symbolic closed shapes; let native metadata select maintained role authority; maintain linear strict children through consuming transitions.
deletion_result: Deleting a dependency forces topology families, eager initialization, universal liveness metadata, permanent tags, scans, per-node roots, or unsafe address authority.
falsifier: The grammar accepts a writer predicate, project identity, arbitrary recursion, or metadata-created owner.

exact_statement: Fixed owner transitions account initialize, take, replace-return-old, swap, overlap-checked relocate, destroy, reclassify, split/join, and exact atomic custody at one logical commit.
derivation: Use exact fragment transfer plus opcode-specific owner multiset equations; attach each recoverable or atomic event arm to a closed outcome.
deletion_result: Deleting a traffic opcode forces extra movement, copy, allocation, transient ownership, stronger overlap restrictions, locks, or project-named operations.
falsifier: A branch invents, loses, duplicates, or restores an owner without the matching predecessor or native event.

exact_statement: Stable, exclusive focus, monotone progress, repair, and poison are closed affine states whose intermediate authority cannot escape as stable.
derivation: Seal the current complete state; consume it into focus or progress; advance a fixed structural measure; close only an exact success, repair, poison, or terminal disposition outcome.
deletion_result: Deleting a dependency forces snapshots, inverse closures, persistent operation tags, mandatory local rollback, arbitrary protocol proofs, or invalid intermediate exposure.
falsifier: A partial or failed path regains Stable while an owner, event, repair, or disposition obligation remains.

exact_statement: Every access leaf retains physical root, layout epoch, local generation, exact interval, access mode, and invalidators across wrapper movement and logical rekeying.
derivation: Normalize exact places and disjoint footprints; borrow only from current live or role authority; escrow incompatible transitions until lexical scope end.
deletion_result: Deleting a dependency forces copies, dynamic borrow tables, coarse whole-root aliasing, per-use root checks, backpointers, or wrong-root acceptance.
falsifier: A wrapper, address, page number, or logical key re-roots a borrow or a loan survives its generation.

exact_statement: Executable disposition consumes only closed spans, classified live sets, bounded strict chains or children, finite products, exact release actions, and admitted affine once callables with a decreasing returning measure.
derivation: Select a closed structural source; remove its affine obligation before a fixed terminal action; use consume-before-invoke for heterogeneous once actions; release only reunited obligation-free roots.
deletion_result: Deleting a dependency forces leaks, full-capacity scans, duplicate destruction, per-container finalizers, runtime tags, boxes, refcounts, or arbitrary cleanup programs.
falsifier: A returning step does not reduce the structural source, an action runs twice, or lowering adds runtime disposition metadata.

exact_statement: Bounds, alignment, live role, same-root, disjointness, current generation, protocol, publication, and noninterference facts are closed read-only projections with exact invalidators.
derivation: Project only from accepted judgments; record all dependencies; invalidate on matching mutation, scope end, interference, relayout, or release; keep facts out of authority premises.
deletion_result: Deleting fact projection retains checks; widening it admits stale or writer-defined optimizer assumptions.
falsifier: Facts-on changes accepted safety authority, facts-off changes semantics, or a fact survives its invalidator.

exact_statement: Atomic custody, protected observation, payload interference, retirement, cutoff validation, and final disposition are distinct resources; no reclamation policy identity is a language form.
derivation: Seal a finite registry and admission profile; create observations only at exact begin events and retired rights only after successful unlink; apply OBS-CLOSE to complete post-cutoff lane coverage; combine one retired right with matching quiescence before disposition.
deletion_result: Deleting a dependency forces global locks, per-load or per-object records, policy-specific forms, arbitrary invariants, duplicate retirement, or premature release.
falsifier: A guard, copied epoch, partial scan, wrapped integer, or writer assertion makes a retired target executable while an old observer remains.
